summary: Explicit lifecycle gate instructions in Twining system prompt instead of vague skill references
context: Full-twining condition's system prompt only said "Use the twining:* skills" — agents had no guidance on which tools to call or when. This meant benchmarks tested "does an agent randomly discover MCP tools" rather than "does Twining's coordination model improve outcomes."
rationale: Replaced with explicit 7-step lifecycle (assemble, why, decide, post, verify, status, handoff) matching real Twining usage. This is the single highest-impact validity fix — without it, results don't measure Twining's intended workflow.

alternatives.option: Keep vague prompt and rely on CLAUDE.md alone
alternatives.reason_rejected: CLAUDE.md also lacked specifics — agents need explicit tool call instructions to reliably engage the lifecycle gates
